methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $26M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Terraform Industries, a Series A climate and energy company founded by Casey Handmer, is valued against a comparables universe of 9 private frontier-tech firms and 1 public peer (LNZA). The company has raised $26M to date.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $26M raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A-to-subsequent-round step-ups, implies a $52M valuation. Peer-median analysis examined three same-sector, same-stage companies: Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M), yielding a $150M median with a range of $110M–$200M. The disparity between methods reflects heterogeneity in capital efficiency and investor appetite across climate-tech.

The two-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $44M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $101M. The wide spread—nearly 4x between low and high—reflects material uncertainty around whether Terraform will command peer median multiples or grow into them. Key variables include proof of unit economics, pathway to commercialization, and competitive positioning within the energy infrastructure stack. The gap between stage-progression ($52M) and peer median ($150M) warrants scrutiny of whether comparables are truly analogous in technical risk, market timing, and capital requirements.
